deck: Day 25 Flashcards (2 - Lab Video) - EIGRP Terms.apkg

cards.front:
Which EIGRP term?
[...] = This router's metric value to the route's destination

Which EIGRP term?
[...] = The neighbor's metric value to the route's destination.

Which EIGRP term?
[...] = The route with the lowest metric to the destination (the best route)

Which EIGRP term?
[...] = An alternate route to the destination which meets the feasibility condition

EIGRP feasibility condition:
A route is considered a [...] if its reported distance is lower than the successor route’s feasible distance.

EIGRP feasibility condition:
A route is considered a feasible successor if its [...] is lower than the successor route’s feasible distance.

EIGRP feasibility condition:
A route is considered a feasible successor if its reported distance is lower than the [...] route’s feasible distance.

EIGRP feasibility condition:
A route is considered a feasible successor if its reported distance is lower than the successor route’s [...].
EIGRP will only perform unequal-cost load-balancing over [...] routes.
If an EIGRP route doesn't meet the feasibility requirement, can it be used for unequal-cost load-balancing?
EIGRP metric calculation uses the bandwidth of [...] plus the delay of [...].
